lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾਂ ਸੁਖੁ ਤਾ ਸਹੁ ਰਾਵਿਓ ਦੁਖਿ ਭੀ ਸੰਮੑਾਲਿਓਇ ॥
lines.translit: jaan sukh taa sahu raavio dukh bhee samaalioe |
lines.english: When there is peace and pleasure, that is the time to remember your Husband Lord. In times of suffering and pain, remember Him then as well.
Second Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਉ ਨ ਮਰੀਜੈ ਰੋਇ ਜਾ ਲਗੁ ਚਿਤਿ ਨ ਆਵਹੀ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: kiau na mareejai roe jaa lag chit na aavahee |1|
lines.english: I might as well just die crying, if You will not come into my mind. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕੁ ਕਹੈ ਸਿਆਣੀਏ ਇਉ ਕੰਤ ਮਿਲਾਵਾ ਹੋਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak kahai siaanee iau kant milaavaa hoe |2|
lines.english: Says Nanak, O wise bride, this is the way to meet your Husband Lord. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉ ਕਿਆ ਸਾਲਾਹੀ ਕਿਰਮ ਜੰਤੁ ਵਡੀ ਤੇਰੀ ਵਡਿਆਈ ॥
lines.translit: hau kiaa saalaahee kiram jant vaddee teree vaddiaaee |
lines.english: I am a worm - how can I praise You, O Lord; Your glorious greatness is so great!
Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੈ ਤੁਝ ਬਿਨੁ ਬੇਲੀ ਕੋ ਨਹੀ ਤੂ ਅੰਤਿ ਸਖਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: mai tujh bin belee ko nahee too ant sakhaaee |
lines.english: I have no other friend except You; in the end, You alone will be my Companion and Support.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂ ਅਗਮ ਦਇਆਲੁ ਅਗੰਮੁ ਹੈ ਆਪਿ ਲੈਹਿ ਮਿਲਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: too agam deaal agam hai aap laihi milaaee |
lines.english: You are inaccessible, merciful and unapproachable; You Yourself unite us with Yourself.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਵੇਪਰਵਾਹੁ ਹੈ ਤਿਸੁ ਤਿਲੁ ਨ ਤਮਾਈ ॥੨੦॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak veparavaahu hai tis til na tamaaee |20|1|
lines.english: O Nanak, He is care-free; He has no greed at all. ||20||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਤੇਰੀ ਸਰਣਾਗਤੀ ਤਿਨ ਲੈਹਿ ਛਡਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: jo teree saranaagatee tin laihi chhaddaaee |
lines.english: You save those who enter Your Sanctuary.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਗੁ ਸੂਹੀ ਬਾਣੀ ਸ੍ਰੀ ਕਬੀਰ ਜੀਉ ਤਥਾ ਸਭਨਾ ਭਗਤਾ ਕੀ ॥ ਕਬੀਰ ਕੇ ॥
lines.translit: raag soohee baanee sree kabeer jeeo tathaa sabhanaa bhagataa kee | kabeer ke |
lines.english: Raag Soohee, The Word Of Kabeer Jee, And Other Devotees. Of Kabeer
One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮ ਕੋ ਨਾਮੁ ਨ ਕਬਹੂ ਲੀਨਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: raam ko naam na kabahoo leenaa |1|
lines.english: You have never even chanted the Name of the Lord. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮ ਨ ਜਪਹੁ ਕਵਨ ਮਤਿ ਲਾਗੇ ॥
lines.translit: raam na japahu kavan mat laage |
lines.english: You have not meditated on the Lord; what thoughts are you attached to?
Since your birth, what have you done?

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਖ ਸੁਖ ਕਰਿ ਕੈ ਕੁਟੰਬੁ ਜੀਵਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: dukh sukh kar kai kuttanb jeevaaeaa |
lines.english: Through pain and pleasure, you have taken care of your family.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਰਿ ਜਇਬੇ ਕਉ ਕਿਆ ਕਰਹੁ ਅਭਾਗੇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: mar jeibe kau kiaa karahu abhaage |1| rahaau |
lines.english: What preparations are you making for your death, O unfortunate one? ||1||Pause||
But at the time of death, you shall have to endure the agony all alone. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਿ ਕਬੀਰ ਆਗੇ ਤੇ ਨ ਸੰਮੑਾਰਾ ॥੩॥੧॥
lines.translit: keh kabeer aage te na samaaraa |3|1|
lines.english: Says Kabeer, why didn't you remember the Lord before this? ||3||1||
When you are seized by the neck, then you shall cry out.
Soohee, Kabeer Jee:
My innocent soul trembles and shakes.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੈਨਿ ਗਈ ਮਤ ਦਿਨੁ ਭੀ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: rain gee mat din bhee jaae |
lines.english: The night of my youth has passed away; will the day of old age also pass away?
I do not know how my Husband Lord will deal with me. ||1||
when the soul-swan departs, the body withers away. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਵਰ ਗਏ ਬਗ ਬੈਠੇ ਆਇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: bhavar ge bag baitthe aae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: My dark hairs, like bumble bees, have gone away, and grey hairs, like cranes, have settled upon my head. ||1||Pause||
Water does not remain in the unbaked clay pot;
My arm is tired, driving away the crows.
I decorate myself like a young virgin;
but how can I enjoy pleasures, without my Husband Lord? ||3||
Your time of service is at its end, and you will have to give your account.
Soohee, Kabeer Jee:
Says Kabeer, this is the way the story of my life ends. ||4||2||
What have you earned, and what have you lost?
The hard-hearted Messenger of Death has come to take you away.
Come immediately! You are summoned to His Court! ||1||
Get going! Come just as you are! You have been summoned to His Court.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਫੁਰਮਾਨੁ ਦਰਗਹ ਕਾ ਆਇਆ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: har furamaan daragah kaa aaeaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: The Order has come from the Court of the Lord. ||1||Pause||
I will collect them tonight;
I pray to the Messenger of Death: please, I still have some outstanding debts to collect in the village.
I will also pay you something for your expenses,
and I will recite my morning prayers on the way. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ ਜਾ ਕਉ ਹਰਿ ਰੰਗੁ ਲਾਗਾ ॥
lines.translit: saadhasang jaa kau har rang laagaa |
lines.english: Blessed, blessed is the most fortunate servant of the Lord,

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਨੁ ਧਨੁ ਸੋ ਜਨੁ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਸਭਾਗਾ ॥
lines.translit: dhan dhan so jan purakh sabhaagaa |
lines.english: Who is imbued with the Lord's Love, in the Saadh Sangat, the Company of the Holy.
They win the priceless treasure of this human life. ||3||
Here and there, the humble servants of the Lord are always happy.
When he is awake, he is sleeping, and so he loses this life.
Says Kabeer, those people are deluded,
who forget their Lord and Master, and roll in the dust. ||4||3||
The property and wealth he has accumulated passes on to someone else.
